---
title: "week7"
author: "chingiz rabdanov"
date: "2026-03-15"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Load packages

library(rvest) library(jsonlite) library(dplyr)

# 1. Load HTML into R data frame

html_url \<-
"<https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Chingiz1492/html-and-json/refs/heads/main/3booksRH.html>"

df_html \<- html_url \|\> read_html() \|\> \# read the HTML page
html_element("table") \|\> \# find the

<table>

tag html_table(header = TRUE) \# convert to data frame, first row =
column names

cat("=== HTML data frame ===\n")

print(df_html)

# 2. Load JSON into a separate R data frame

json_url \<-
"<https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Chingiz1492/html-and-json/refs/heads/main/3booksRH.json>"

df_json_raw \<- fromJSON(json_url) \# read JSON directly into data frame
cat("=== JSON data frame (raw) ===\n")

print(df_json)

# 3. Fix the three differences in JSON

# Fix 1: Convert Authors from list to string

# BEFORE: list("Orlando Figes")

# AFTER: "Orlando Figes"

df_json_raw$Authors <- sapply(
  df_json_raw$Authors, function(x) paste(trimws(x), collapse = ", ") )

# Fix 2: Rename "Date of publication" → "Date_of_publication"

# BEFORE: "Date of publication"

# AFTER: "Date_of_publication"

names(df_json_raw)[names(df_json_raw) == "Date of publication"] \<-
"Date_of_publication"

# Fix 3: Replace NA with "none" in ISBN_10

# BEFORE: NA

# AFTER: "none"

df_json_raw$ISBN_10[is.na(df_json_raw$ISBN_10)] \<- "none"

df_json \<- df_json_raw

cat("=== JSON data frame (fixed) ===\n") print(df_json)

# 4. Normalize both data frames for comparison

# Align column order in JSON to match HTML

df_json \<- df_json[, names(df_html)]

# Reset row numbers in both data frames

rownames(df_html) \<- NULL rownames(df_json) \<- NULL

# Convert all columns to character to remove type differences

df_html_norm \<- df_html \|\> mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
df_json_norm \<- df_json \|\> mutate(across(everything(), as.character))

# 5. Compare using identical()

result \<- identical(df_html_norm, df_json_norm)

if (result) { cat("\n✅ identical() = TRUE: data frames are fully
identical\n") } else { cat("\n❌ identical() = FALSE: data frames still
differ\n")

\# Show exactly which column differs cat("\nColumn-by-column
comparison:\n") for (col in names(df_html_norm)) { match \<-
identical(df_html_norm[[col]], df_json_norm[[col]]) cat(" -", col, ":",
ifelse(match, "✅ match", "❌ differ"), "\n") } }

# 6. Final output

cat("\n=== FINAL: HTML data frame ===\n") print(df_html)

cat("\n=== FINAL: JSON data frame ===\n") print(df_json)
